metadata.name: tribe_voter_weight
metadata.description: The Tribe — voter weight plugin: locked shares, delegation, analyst registry

docs: # The Tribe — Voter Weight Plugin
Supplies SPL Governance's `VoterWeightRecord` / `MaxVoterWeightRecord` addin interface
with Tribe's voting model: locked + delegated shares carry power, passive shares don't,
and only registered analysts holding >=1% of active power may create proposals.
spl-governance never CPIs an addin — it deserializes and checks whatever
VoterWeightRecord/MaxVoterWeightRecord account the caller supplies. So every
instruction this program exposes is **user-callable**, meant to run in the same
transaction as the spl-governance instruction (CastVote, CreateProposal, ...) it backs.
Three invariants that must NEVER be broken (invariant 1 enforced starting phase 03,
once VoterWeightRecord/MaxVoterWeightRecord exist; 2 and 3 from phase 03/04):
1. Σ Locker.amount_locked == Σ VoterWeightRecord.voter_weight == MaxVoterWeightRecord.
max_voter_weight, at all times. A drift here means governance is voting on a
number that doesn't match anyone's actual locked shares.
2. voter_weight_expiry is never open-ended. A record must expire same-slot, or a
member could vote (or propose) using power computed before they unlocked.
3. The analyst/proposal gate fails closed. Any missing, unregistered, or
below-threshold case yields zero CreateProposal weight — never default-allow.

instructions.name: cancel_unlock
docs: Cancels part or all of a still-pending unlock: no token movement, no new timer,
voting power restored immediately (phase 03).

accounts.name: registrar
docs: Must match `locker.registrar` explicitly — unlike lock_shares/request_unlock,
Locker's own seeds here derive from `locker.registrar` rather than
`registrar.key()`, so nothing else ties this account to the locker. Without this
check a caller could supply an unrelated registrar and credit/debit the wrong
realm's VoterWeightRecord/MaxVoterWeightRecord.

instructions.name: create_locker
docs: One per (registrar, member). Self-delegates by default.

accounts.name: escrow
docs: Escrow holding this member's locked shares. Authority is the Locker PDA itself —
it can sign CPIs via its own seeds, so no separate authority PDA is needed.

instructions.name: create_registrar
docs: One per realm. Fixes `unlock_wait_seconds`/`analyst_stake_lamports` from the
program's own audited constants — not from `realm`, which is only an identity.

instructions.name: create_voter_weight_record
docs: Every member who can RECEIVE delegation (including self-delegation) needs one of
these before shares can be locked to them.

instructions.name: deregister_analyst
docs: Refunds the stake and closes the record, unless the analyst is the proposer of a
still-live proposal (see this instruction's doc comment in analyst.rs for exactly
what the live-proposal guard does and doesn't prove).

instructions.name: lock_shares
docs: Moves `amount` shares from the owner into escrow custody. Voting power (phase 03)
accrues to `locker.delegate` immediately.

accounts.name: delegate_vwr
docs: The locker's CURRENT delegate's weight record — defaults to the owner's own
record (self-delegation) unless `set_delegate` has changed it.

instructions.name: register_analyst
docs: Open to anyone, backed by a refundable stake (README: requiring approval would
deadlock at launch). The stake is the only spam deterrent — the real gate on who
may propose is the >=1% active-power check in `update_voter_weight_record`.

accounts.name: analyst_record
docs: Stake lives directly on this account's own lamport balance (on top of what `init`
reserves for rent) — no separate escrow PDA. `deregister_analyst`'s `close =
analyst` refunds the whole balance (rent + stake) in one step, nothing to
reconcile between two accounts.

instructions.name: request_unlock
docs: Starts the two-stage unlock: voting power is removed immediately (phase 03), the
shares themselves become withdrawable only after `unlock_wait_seconds`.

instructions.name: set_delegate
docs: Moves a locker's realized weight from its current delegate to a new one. The new
delegate must already have a VoterWeightRecord.

accounts.name: old_delegate_vwr
docs: The Locker's CURRENT delegate's record, read before this instruction mutates
`locker.delegate` — Anchor validates this seed against the on-chain `locker` state
as it exists at the start of the instruction, which is still the old delegate.

accounts.name: new_delegate_vwr
docs: The new delegate's record. Must already exist — Anchor's own account
deserialization enforces this (an uninitialized account cannot load as
`Account<'info, VoterWeightRecord>`), so no separate "record missing" error is
needed here.

instructions.name: update_max_voter_weight_record
docs: Permissionless — refreshes the realm's MaxVoterWeightRecord expiry before a quorum
check. `max_voter_weight` itself is already a live running total.

instructions.name: update_voter_weight_record
docs: Refreshes a record's expiry/action/target for a specific governance action, in the
same transaction as the spl-governance instruction it backs. Does not recompute
`voter_weight` — that's a live running total already.

accounts.name: max_vwr
docs: Always required (it's a cheap, always-existing realm singleton) even though it's
only read for the CreateProposal branch — simpler than making it conditionally
optional for one specific action.

accounts.name: analyst_record
docs: Only present for analysts. `None` for everyone else — checked only when
`weight_action == CreateProposal`; irrelevant (and fine to omit) for CastVote and
every other action.

instructions.name: withdraw_unlocked
docs: Releases the pending amount to the owner once the wait has elapsed.

errors.name: AmountZero
errors.msg: Amount must be greater than zero

errors.name: InsufficientLocked
errors.msg: Locked amount is insufficient for this request

errors.name: UnlockNotElapsed
errors.msg: Unlock wait period has not elapsed yet

errors.name: NothingToWithdraw
errors.msg: No pending unlock amount to withdraw

errors.name: InsufficientPending
errors.msg: Pending unlock amount is insufficient to cancel this much

errors.name: MintMismatch
errors.msg: Mint does not match the registrar's governing token mint

errors.name: TokenAccountOwnerMismatch
errors.msg: Token account owner does not match the expected signer

errors.name: WeightUnderflow
errors.msg: Weight underflow: delegate's voter weight or the realm's max voter weight would go negative

errors.name: RealmMismatch
errors.msg: Registrar does not match this locker's registrar

errors.name: AlreadyDelegated
errors.msg: New delegate is already the current delegate

errors.name: NotRegisteredAnalyst
errors.msg: Owner is not a registered analyst

errors.name: BelowProposalThreshold
errors.msg: Analyst holds less than 1% of active voting power

errors.name: NotSplGovernanceAccount
errors.msg: A passed proposal/token-owner-record account is not owned by spl-governance

errors.name: DeregisterBlocked
errors.msg: Cannot deregister while proposer of a live proposal

types.name: AnalystRecord
docs: One per registered analyst. Independent from Locker — a member may be an analyst
without locking any shares, and may lock shares without ever registering. Registration
is deliberately open (README: requiring approval would deadlock at launch — approval
needs votes, votes need shares, shares need someone to have deposited first). The
refundable stake is the only spam deterrent; the 1% active-power threshold (checked at
CreateProposal time, not here) is what actually gates who can propose.

fields.name: stake_lamports
docs: Exact lamports posted at registration — refunded exactly this amount on
deregistration, tracked separately from the account's rent so the two can never be
confused.

types.name: Locker
docs: One per (registrar, member). Tracks a member's locked shares, any shares mid-unlock,
and who currently gets voting credit for them.
Invariant that must hold after every instruction touching this account:
`amount_locked + pending_unlock_amount == escrow.amount`. The escrow is the only place
shares physically sit once locked — this account just books who they belong to and
whether they're still voting or on their way out.

fields.name: owner
docs: The member who owns these shares. Only this key may lock/unlock/cancel/withdraw.

fields.name: delegate
docs: Who currently receives voting credit for `amount_locked`. Defaults to `owner`
(self-delegation) at `create_locker` — shares are never voteless by default, but
they ARE powerless until locked, per the README's voting model.

fields.name: amount_locked
docs: Shares currently locked and counted in `delegate`'s voting weight.

fields.name: pending_unlock_amount
docs: Shares that had `request_unlock` called on them: voting power already removed,
principal not yet released. Zero means nothing is mid-unlock.

fields.name: unlock_available_at
docs: Unix timestamp `withdraw_unlocked` becomes callable at. Meaningless (and ignored)
while `pending_unlock_amount == 0`.
NOTE: a second `request_unlock` while a pending amount already exists resets this
to `now + unlock_wait_seconds` for the COMBINED pending balance — there is no
per-request queue, by design (one scalar timer, matching the account's fixed
size). A member who wants independent timers must fully withdraw before
requesting again.

fields.name: escrow_bump
docs: Bump of this locker's escrow token account (seeds derive from this account's own
key, so the escrow's address only exists once this account does).

types.name: MaxVoterWeightRecord
docs: SPL Governance's max-voter-weight addin interface account — one per realm, the running
total of every VoterWeightRecord's weight in this realm. Required because Tribe's
quorum is a PERCENTAGE of active voting power (30%, per the passing rule), so
spl-governance needs a denominator. Same hand-defined-struct rationale as
VoterWeightRecord above; same "do not add an explicit discriminator field" warning.

fields.name: max_voter_weight
docs: Running total: `Σ VoterWeightRecord.voter_weight == max_voter_weight ==
Σ Locker.amount_locked` must hold at all times. Mutated by the same
credit_delegate/debit_delegate calls as the delegate's VoterWeightRecord, never
independently.

types.name: Registrar
docs: One per realm. Defines the governing token (Tribe's share mint) and the fixed
unlock-wait / analyst-stake parameters this realm's members are locked into.
`unlock_wait_seconds`/`analyst_stake_lamports` are copied here from the audited
program constants (`UNLOCK_WAIT_SECONDS`/`ANALYST_STAKE_LAMPORTS`) at creation time —
NOT caller-supplied — so every Locker/AnalystRecord in this realm can read its config
locally without a second account, while the actual security parameters stay fixed by
the program, never settable by whoever calls `create_registrar`.

fields.name: realm
docs: The SPL Governance realm this registrar backs. Not independently verified against
spl-governance in this phase — wiring that check is phase 05's integration-test
concern, once a real realm exists to check against.

fields.name: governing_token_mint
docs: Tribe's share mint. Must equal the vault's `share_mint` (checked at every lock).

fields.name: realm_authority
docs: Whoever created this registrar. Recorded for future authority-gated operations;
today only `create_registrar`'s one-time init reads it.

fields.name: unlock_wait_seconds
docs: Copied from `UNLOCK_WAIT_SECONDS` at creation. See that constant for rationale.

fields.name: analyst_stake_lamports
docs: Copied from `ANALYST_STAKE_LAMPORTS` at creation. See that constant for rationale.

types.name: VoterWeightAction
docs: The governance action a VoterWeightRecord's weight was computed for. Mirrors
spl-governance-addin-api's `VoterWeightAction` enum EXACTLY — variant order is part of
the wire format (Borsh encodes enum variants by index, not name), so this must never be
reordered or have a variant inserted anywhere but the end.

types.name: VoterWeightRecord
docs: SPL Governance's voter-weight addin interface account — hand-defined locally rather
than depending on `spl-governance-addin-api` (see phase 01/03 notes: that crate cannot
compile for the Solana BPF/SBF target at all). Anchor's own `#[account]` macro
auto-derives and strips an 8-byte discriminator = sha256("account:VoterWeightRecord")
[..8] on every serialize/deserialize — independently verified (computed in Python) to
equal spl-governance's hardcoded expected bytes for this exact struct name. Do NOT add
an explicit `account_discriminator` field here: Anchor already prepends one, and a
second explicit copy would shift every field after it and break the byte layout
spl-governance expects.
`voter_weight` is a **running total**, not computed on demand: `weight.rs`'s
credit_delegate/debit_delegate (called from lock.rs's lock_shares/request_unlock/cancel_unlock)
keep it in sync with the sum of every Locker currently delegating to this owner.

fields.name: governing_token_owner
docs: The voter this record's weight belongs to (the delegate, not necessarily anyone
who has locked shares themselves).

fields.name: voter_weight_expiry
docs: Must be `Some(current_slot)` whenever this record is used, never left open-ended —
an unbounded expiry would let a member vote (or propose) using power computed
before they unlocked. Set only by `update_voter_weight_record`, same-tx as the
governance action it backs (spl-governance does not CPI the addin, so this record
must already be fresh when spl-governance reads it).

constants.name: MAX_VWR_SEED
docs: One MaxVoterWeightRecord per (realm, mint) — a realm-wide singleton.

constants.name: VWR_SEED
docs: Seed convention chosen by Tribe (not protocol-enforced by spl-governance — it only
deserializes+checks whatever account the caller supplies, seeds are addin-side
convention only). One VoterWeightRecord per (realm, mint, owner) — "owner" here is
whoever can RECEIVE delegation, not necessarily anyone who has locked shares.
